summary: Renamed several previously anonymous HSD controller/pad functions in baselib assembly and updated C/assembly callsites to use the new HSD_Pad* labels. The PR primarily improved symbol clarity around controller initialization, status renewal, clamp checks, AD conversion checks, cross-direction handling, and rumble initialization without changing behavior.

key_files.role: Main controller assembly file where multiple func_8037xxxx symbols were renamed to HSD_Pad* labels and internal branch targets/calls were updated.
Updated assembly callsites from anonymous controller functions to HSD_PadRenewMasterStatus and HSD_PadRenewCopyStatus.
Promoted the commented guess for func_80378828 into the actual function name HSD_PadRumbleInit.
Updated game main initialization to call HSD_PadInit instead of func_80377D98.

what_changed: Renamed func_80376E48 to HSD_PadClampCheck1 in controller.s.
Renamed func_80376E90 to HSD_PadClampCheck3 in controller.s.
Renamed func_803771D4 to HSD_PadADConvertCheck1 in controller.s.
Renamed func_80377450 to HSD_PadCrossDir in controller.s.
Renamed func_8037750C to HSD_PadRenewMasterStatus in controller.s and updated callsites in lbunknown_005.s and controller.s.
Renamed func_803779C0 to HSD_PadRenewCopyStatus in controller.s and updated callsites in lbunknown_005.s and controller.s.
Renamed func_80377D98 to HSD_PadInit in controller.s and updated the gmmain.c callsite.
Renamed func_80378828 to HSD_PadRumbleInit in rumble.c and updated the controller.s callsite.
Removed the stale comment-only label marker '// HSD_PadRumbleInit' once the rumble init function was actually named.
smart_moves: Applied labels consistently at both definition sites and callsites, which keeps assembly and C references searchable and avoids a split between old func_* names and new HSD names.
Used the existing baselib-style HSD_Pad prefix, matching already-known symbols such as HSD_PadLibData and HSD_PadRumbleListData.
Converted a known comment hint in rumble.c into a real symbol name, improving type-aware C callsites and downstream decompilation readability.
Renamed related controller helper functions as a coherent group rather than only labeling externally visible initialization functions.
decomp_lessons: When labeling library code, update every direct branch/call reference in assembly slices so the symbol graph becomes useful for future matching and navigation.
Commented suspected function names should be promoted to actual symbols once confidence is sufficient, then the redundant comment should be removed.
HSD/baselib functions should use the SDK-like prefix and PascalCase component naming when evidence supports it, e.g. HSD_PadInit and HSD_PadRumbleInit.
Even small no-behavior renames are valuable in decompilation because they clarify subsystem boundaries and make later C migrations easier.

naming_conventions.lesson: Controller-related baselib functions were labeled with the HSD_Pad prefix and descriptive PascalCase suffixes.

naming_conventions.pattern: Replace func_80xxxxxx placeholders at definitions and callsites
naming_conventions.lesson: Do not leave old anonymous func_* references at callsites once a function has an accepted label.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Assembly label replacement preserved instruction bytes and branch offsets; the diff shows symbolic call target changes only, not behavioral instruction changes.
Internal calls inside controller.s were relabeled to the new local function names, e.g. branch-and-link calls to HSD_PadClampCheck1, HSD_PadClampCheck3, HSD_PadADConvertCheck1, and HSD_PadCrossDir.
Cross-file assembly references in lbunknown_005.s were updated to use HSD_PadRenewMasterStatus and HSD_PadRenewCopyStatus, improving linkage clarity across subsystems.
C callsites were updated where decompiled source already existed, specifically gmmain.c calling HSD_PadInit and controller.s calling the C-defined HSD_PadRumbleInit.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile asm/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.s into C using the HSD_Pad* labels from PR 508.
Search for remaining func_8037xxxx symbols in sysdolphin/baselib/controller.s after this PR.
Search for usages of HSD_PadClampCheck1, HSD_PadClampCheck3, and HSD_PadADConvertCheck1 to confirm parameter meanings and potential struct fields.
Look for subsequent header additions declaring HSD_PadInit and HSD_PadRumbleInit.
